summary.text: Valid ARIA role values.
ARIA text value: a string or falsy value.
ARIA text input: static text or a reactive State.
ARIA role input: static role or a reactive State.
ARIA boolean input: static boolean or a reactive State.
ARIA mixed boolean value: true, false, "mixed", or falsy.
ARIA mixed boolean input: static value or a reactive State.
ARIA current value: true, or a specific page location type.
ARIA current input: static value or a reactive State.
ARIA live region politeness level.
ARIA live input: static value or a reactive State.
ARIA reference: an element ID string, HTMLElement, component with element, or falsy.
ARIA reference selection: a single reference or iterable of references.
ARIA reference input: static selection or a reactive State.

Fluent builder for setting ARIA attributes on an element.

All methods accept static values or reactive States (State).
Methods return the owning Component to enable fluent chaining.
Internally uses an AttributeManipulator to apply changes.
Set the ARIA role.
The role value or reactive State.
Set the ARIA label.
The label text or reactive State.
Set the ARIA description.
The description text or reactive State.
Set the ARIA role description.
The role description text or reactive State.
Set aria-labelledby: elements that label this element.
Element reference(s) or reactive State.
Set aria-describedby: elements that describe this element.
Element reference(s) or reactive State.
Set aria-controls: elements controlled by this element.
Element reference(s) or reactive State.
Set aria-details: elements that provide details for this element.
Element reference(s) or reactive State.
Set aria-owns: elements owned by this element.
Element reference(s) or reactive State.
Set aria-flowto: elements that follow this element.
Element reference(s) or reactive State.
Set aria-hidden: whether this element is hidden from assistive technology.
The boolean value or reactive State.
Set aria-disabled: whether this element is disabled.
The boolean value or reactive State.
Set aria-expanded: whether this element is expanded.
The boolean value or reactive State.
Set aria-busy: whether this element is busy/loading.
The boolean value or reactive State.
Set aria-selected: whether this element is selected.
The boolean value or reactive State.
Set aria-checked: whether this element is checked (true, false, or "mixed").
The boolean/mixed value or reactive State.
Set aria-pressed: whether this element is pressed (true, false, or "mixed").
The boolean/mixed value or reactive State.
Set aria-current: mark this element or one of its descendants as the current page/step/location.
The current value (true, false, or a location type) or reactive State.
Set aria-live: announce dynamic content updates (off, polite, or assertive).
The politeness level or reactive State.

Manages CSS class application to an HTML element with support for static values,
reactive state, and multiple overlapping style determiners.

Each class has a single "determiner" — the most recent operation (add/remove/bind)
takes precedence. When a determiner is replaced, it is properly cleaned up.

for that class. Falsy values and values in iterables are ignored.

when the user's operating system or browser is set to a dark color scheme.
CSS properties to apply under the dark color scheme.
Creates a spreadable
`@container scroll-state(...)`

wrapper that applies styles
when a scroll container is stuck on any edge (
`left`
`right`
`top`
, or
`bottom`

).

This uses the CSS Scroll-Driven Animations
`scroll-state`
query syntax:
`@container scroll-state((stuck: left) or (stuck: right) or (stuck: top) or (stuck: bottom))`
CSS properties to apply when the container is stuck.

Creates a spreadable pseudo-class selector that matches when the element is in an open state,
such as a
`<details>`

element that is toggled open.
Produces the CSS pseudo-class
`:open`

Creates a spreadable pseudo-class selector that matches when the element is in a closed state,
such as a
`<details>`

element that is toggled closed.
Produces the CSS pseudo-class
`:not(:open)`
CSS properties to apply when the element is closed.

State.extend
to access and modify the State prototype.

Abstract base class for managing cleanup functions and resource disposal.
Owners can register cleanup functions that are invoked when the owner is disposed,
enabling a predictable lifecycle for dependent resources.
Whether this owner has been disposed.

Disposes this owner and invokes all registered cleanup functions.
Once disposed, an owner cannot be used again.
Subsequent calls to
`dispose()`
are no-ops.

Registers a cleanup function to be invoked when this owner is disposed.
If the owner is already disposed, the cleanup function is invoked immediately.

content.text: A function that unregisters the cleanup function. Calling it prevents the cleanup function from being invoked later.

Subscribes to synchronous state changes without binding to an owner.
The listener is invoked immediately (synchronously) whenever the state value changes.
Use this for quick derivations and computed values. If the state is disposed, returns a no-op unsubscribe function.
Function called with (newValue, previousValue) on each change.

Subscribes to asynchronous state changes without binding to an owner.
Listeners are batched and invoked together in microtasks, receiving only the original and final values.
Multiple state changes between listener invocations are coalesced.
Use this for side effects that can tolerate slight delays. If the state is disposed, returns a no-op unsubscribe function.
Function called with (finalValue, originalValue) after batched changes.

Subscribes to synchronous state changes with automatic cleanup via an owner.
The listener is invoked immediately (synchronously) whenever the state value changes.
The subscription is automatically cleaned up when the owner is disposed.

Creates a reactive state container with an initial value.

When called with an owner, the state is automatically disposed when the owner is disposed.

When called without an owner, the state must gain an owner before the next tick.
If the ownerless state is subscribed to by a non-State owner (e.g., a Component),
that owner becomes the implicit owner. If a different non-State owner later subscribes,
the implicit owner is cleared and an explicit owner is required.

readme.text:
# kitsui

kitsui is a DOM-first UI library built around owned

is an owned reactive value.

## Preferred Style

- Write small factory functions that return a

.
- Build most structure in one fluent chain, then use targeted manipulators for classes, attributes, and ARIA.
- Create

inside the component or owner that should dispose it.
- Use derived state and conditional helpers instead of manual DOM bookkeeping.
- Prefer

objects over raw class strings once styling becomes reusable.

## A Typical Component
owns the state and child components, so removing

also disposes the whole subtree.

## State-Driven Rendering

Use
when you already have a component and want state to mutate it. Use
with a stateful component selection when the state should swap whole child selections.

when a specific child should be shown and hidden without being recreated each time. kitsui parks the node off-DOM until it is shown again, and disposes it when the returned cleanup runs or the owner is removed.

## Placement And Movement
move an existing component relative to another component, node, or placement marker. Their
is the low-level escape hatch when the location itself is reactive.
creates reusable style definition fragments that can be spread into multiple class definitions to reduce duplication.
manages both valueless and valued attributes, including reactive names and values.
provides typed wrappers around common ARIA attributes such as role, label, references, booleans,

.

## Derived State

The mapping extension is central to succinct code:

-
creates a derived state owned by

creates a grouped state that snapshots multiple states and emits one next-tick update per tick.

Prefer these over ad hoc listeners when the result is still state.
: typed ARIA helpers layered on top of attributes
- ARIA: the entrypoint also re-exports the typed ARIA unions and inputs used by

instances from factory functions.
- Let ownership do cleanup work for you.
- Use

for local view updates and derived state for reusable logic.
- Reach for
and stateful child selections before manual

plus DOM mutation.
- Keep low-level DOM access for event handlers and genuinely custom behavior; let kitsui handle lifecycle and placement.
